sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 4.41 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.4)

statement.latex: A point$z$in the complex plane is called free\/ if the matrices {$( {cc}1&2\\0&1 )$} and {$( {cc}1&0\ &1 )$} generate a free group. Are all points outside the rhombus with vertices$2$,$i$free? { Yu.\,I.\,Merzlyakov} No (Yu.\,A.\,Ignatov, Math. USSR--Sb., { 35} (1979), 49--56; \ A.\,I.\,Shkuratski , Math. Notes, { 24} (1978), 720--721).

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
